message:
style: collapse nested if-let + hoist clippy allow to crate-level in benches

Two small, unrelated-but-mechanical cleanups kept out of the feature
commits so those can stay focused.

src/decode/async_decode.rs
- Two `if let Some(..) { if cond { body } }` ladders collapsed into
  Rust 2024 `if let Some(..) && cond { body }` form — clippy's
  `collapsible_match` / `if_let_chain` nudge, no behavior change.
  Touches the initial-line array-header detection in
  process_initial_line and the depth-change handling in the main
  event-processing loop.

benches/toon_benchmark.rs
- Hoist the two per-function `#[allow(clippy::cast_precision_loss)]`
  attributes on `generate_tabular_array` and `bench_compression_ratio`
  to a single crate-level `#![allow(clippy::cast_precision_loss)]`.
  The bench crate casts `usize` row counts to f64 for throughput
  calculations in most functions; the central allow is simpler and
  avoids adding the same attribute to every new bench.
